description: Validate a .vue change through SFC compile, focused tests, and browser interaction.
body: Use when editing Vue single-file components. Avoid relying only on string inspection. Steps: inspect template/script/style blocks, identify reactive owners, run package test/typecheck/build script that compiles SFCs, then browser-smoke changed states when visible. Verify console is clean and affected interactions work. Risk boundary: scoped styles and slots may fail only in rendered context. Use when Vue Frontend Engineering work touches visible UI behavior, browser console output, DOM state, assets, viewport behavior, and interaction evidence. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: follow the ordered review, edit, and validation sequence; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include browser smoke, component tests, viewport screenshots, console/network checks, and accessibility assertions. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves visual regressions, inaccessible interaction, hydration mismatch, and browser-only failures, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
